name: Operational Practice and Policy Search for Synthetic Response Use

search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for targeted query

search_queries.query: "Synthetic responses generated using large language models"
search_queries.result_count: confirmed existing New Mexico operational source
screened first result page for targeted query
screened first result page for targeted query

search_queries.query: New Meridian Standard Processes simulated responses test suite machine scoring
Maine Science Assessment 2024 Technical Report simulated student responses machine-scored output
Augmenting AI scoring of essays with GPT-generated responses
"Using GPT-4 to Augment Imbalanced Data for Automatic Scoring"
search_queries.result_count: metadata and preprint located; deferred as redundant with included direct augmentation sources

inclusion_criteria: Direct sources must mention synthetic, simulated, generated, or GPT-created student/learner responses in an automated scoring, machine-scoring, scoring QA, scorer development, or validation-related workflow.
Operational reports, state technical reports, vendor/process guidance, and peer-reviewed studies with full text were prioritized.
The source must support a specific workflow role, such as training augmentation, scoring-rule test suites, item authoring/review QA, stress testing, or validation boundary-setting.
Claims about high-stakes or operational use require primary operational documentation or explicit assessment-program context.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude generic automated scoring studies that do not include synthetic or simulated response corpora.
Exclude generic AI policy unless it directly changes a synthetic-response workflow boundary.
Exclude psychometric simulations that do not involve response artifacts used in scorer development, scoring QA, or automated-scoring evaluation.
Do not treat rule-based machine-scoring test suites as evidence for LLM automated scorer validation.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by title, DOI, official technical-report URL, existing source_id, and whether the source added a new workflow role.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 40 records across targeted web search, official reports, and existing graph audit

screening_decisions.title: Standard Processes
screening_decisions.reason: Direct operational process guidance for simulated responses as machine-scoring test-suite cases that validate scoring and scanning rules.

screening_decisions.title: Maine Science Assessment Technical Report Spring 2024
screening_decisions.reason: Official state technical report documenting simulated student responses and machine-scored output review as native to the item authoring/review process.

screening_decisions.title: Augmenting AI Scoring of Essays with GPT-Generated Responses
screening_decisions.reason: Peer-reviewed direct evidence for GPT-generated essays as AI scoring training augmentation with held-out real-student evaluation and subgroup analyses.

screening_decisions.title: 2025 New Mexico MSSA Technical Report
screening_decisions.reason: Already published in the existing graph; used as contextual operational training-augmentation comparator rather than restaged in this bundle.

screening_decisions.title: 2020-2021 Dynamic Learning Maps Technical Manual Update: Reliability
screening_decisions.reason: Uses simulated item responses for reliability simulation, but not as synthetic student-response evidence for automated scorer training, scoring-rule QA, or scorer validation.

screening_decisions.title: Using GPT-4 to Augment Imbalanced Data for Automatic Scoring
screening_decisions.reason: Direct automatic-scoring augmentation source, but deferred because the bundle already includes Zhang et al. and existing Djagba evidence; a later pass can reconcile the arXiv preprint and Journal of Educational Measurement version.

screening_decisions.title: Maine Science Assessment Technical Report Spring 2023
screening_decisions.reason: Shows similar simulated-response machine-scoring review language, but the 2024 report is newer and sufficient for the operational state-report finding.

screening_summary: This targeted operational-practice pass found a clearer acceptable-use category that is distinct from LLM training augmentation: simulated responses are documented in operational machine-scoring workflows as scoring-rule, scanning-rule, and item-authoring QA/test-suite material. The pass also adds a peer-reviewed GPT essay augmentation study that strengthens the research-development training-augmentation lane while preserving real-response evaluation and human-review limitations.
limitations: This was a targeted surveillance pass, not a systematic review of all state technical reports or vendors.
The operational QA sources concern rule-based machine-scored items, not LLM-generated constructed responses or automated essay scoring validation.
The Zhang et al. source is direct research evidence, not an operational policy or deployment report.
The search does not resolve whether additional vendors or states allow LLM-generated responses for scorer training, validation, monitoring, or live scoring.
